                                                                                                 Functions

Time Series Functions

                       Time series functions enable you to aggregate and forecast data based on time dimensions.
                       For example, you might use the AGO function to calculate revenue from one year ago.

                       Time dimension members must be at or below the level of the function. Because of this, one or
                       more columns that uniquely identify members at or below the given level must be projected in
                       the query.

Function  Example           Description                                         Syntax
AGO
          SELECT Year_ID,   Calculates the aggregated value of a measure in AGO(MEASURE, TIME_LEVEL,
          AGO(sales, year,
          1)                a specified time period in the past. For example, OFFSET)

                            to calculate monthly revenue one year ago, use      Where:
                            AGO(Revenue, Year, 1, SHIP_MONTH). To
                            calculate quarterly revenues in the last quarter,   · MEASURE represents the
                            use AGO(Revenue, Quarter, 1).                             measure to calculate, for
                                                                                      example, revenue.

                                                                                · TIME_LEVEL represents the

                                                                                time interval, which must be

                                                                                Year, Quarter, Month, Week, or

                                                                                Day.

                                                                                · OFFSET represents the

                                                                                number of time intervals to

                                                                                calculate back to, for example,

                                                                                1 for one year.

PERIODR   SELECT Month_ID,  Calculates the aggregate of a measure over the PERIODROLLING(measure, x
OLLING    PERIODROLLING
          (monthly_sales,   period starting x units of time and ending y units [,y])
          -1, 1)
                            of time from the current time. For example,         Where:

                            PERIODROLLING can compute sales for a period        · MEASURE represents the

                            that starts at a quarter before and ends at a       name of a measure column.

                            quarter after the current quarter.                  · X is an integer that represents

                                                                                the offset from the current time.

                                                                                · Y is an integer that represents

                                                                                the number of time units over

                                                                                which the function calculates.

                                                                                · HIERARCHY is an optional

                                                                                argument that represents the

                                                                                name of a hierarchy in a time

                                                                                dimension such as YR, MON,

                                                                                DAY, that you want to use to

                                                                                compute the time window.

TODATE    SELECT Year_ID,   Calculates the aggregated value of a measure        TODATE(MEASURE, TIME_LEVEL)
          Month_ID, TODATE  from the start of a time period to the latest time
          (sales, year)     period, for example, year to date calculations.     Where:

                            For example, to calculate Year to Date Sales,       · MEASURE represents an
                            use TODATE(sales, year).                                  expression that references at
                                                                                      least one measure column, for
                                                                                      example, sales.

                                                                                · TIME_LEVEL represents the
                                                                                      time interval, which must be
                                                                                      Year, Quarter, Month, Week, or
                                                                                      Day.

                                                                           Constants

Constants

                       You can use constants to include specific fixed dates and times in workbooks and reports.

Constant   Example      Description                                        Syntax
DATE                                                                       DATE 'yyyy-mm-dd'
TIME       DATE '2026-04-09' Creates a specific date in a calculation or
TIMESTAMP                                 expression.                      TIME 'hh:mi:ss'

           TIME '12:00:00' Creates a specific time in a calculation or     TIMESTAMP 'yyyy-mm-dd
                                          expression.                      hh:mi:ss'

           TIMESTAMP    Creates a specific time-stamp in a calculation or
           '2026-04-09  expression.
           12:00:00'

Types

                       You can use data types, such as CHAR, INT, and NUMERIC in expressions.
                       For example, you use types when creating CAST expressions that change the data type of an
                       expression or a null literal to another data type.

Variables

           Variables are used in expressions.
           You can use a variable in an expression.
           See Advanced Techniques: Reference Stored Values in Variables.
